metadata.title: María Luisa de Mateo — Mexican Artist | Realist Painting
metadata.description: Portfolio of María Luisa de Mateo Venturini, Mexican visual artist. Realist oil painting: portraits, abstract realism, landscapes and still life. UNAM · UC Berkeley · 30+ exhibitions.

hero.title: Art is the lie that enables us to realize the truth
hero.subtitle: A curated collection of works exploring light, form, and the human condition.

bio.label: Biography
bio.title: María Luisa de Mateo
bio.p1: The painting of María Luisa de Mateo Venturini is realist for the simple pleasure of recreating moments of reality, textures of reality, emotions of reality, immersion in the colors of reality. Far from hyperrealism, very close to abstract realism where reality is built through brushstrokes, through colors.
bio.p2: Born in Mexico City. She studied History with a specialization in Art at UNAM, graduating with honors. She completed graduate studies in Art History at the University of California, Berkeley, USA.
bio.p3: In a much-enjoyed detour, she studied a master's in Economics at UNAM, also with honors, even though she always knew her passion was painting.
bio.p4: Without patience for formal schooling, she studies, researches, and learns from the great masters, particularly Velázquez, whom she copies and dissects. Perhaps due to her training as a historian, her specialty and greatest joy is portraiture.
bio.p5: Even though any exercise with a brush in hand is equally gratifying. Her paintings come in different formats, primarily oil on canvas, though she has also produced engravings and serigraphs.
bio.p6: She has held exhibitions both in Mexico and abroad. The different themes in her paintings are expressions of these exhibitions.
